observations: SCOPE/NON_GOALS UNCHANGED, CONFIRMED PROGRAMMATICALLY: parsed both revision 0 (reconstructed verbatim from this review's own round-1 transcript) and revision 1 (.agent/contracts/031.json on disk) as JSON and diffed the `scope`, `non_goals`, `name`, `spec_ref`, and `estimated_lines` fields directly: all identical (`scope identical: True`, `non_goals identical: True`, `estimated_lines identical: True`). This closes the coordinator's 'confirm scope/non_goals truly unchanged' requirement with a structural diff, not a re-read of design_notes' own claim.

ELEVEN OF THE ORIGINAL THIRTEEN VERIFICATION COMMANDS ARE BYTE-IDENTICAL: extracted the exact command strings for original entries 1, 2, 4, 5, 6, 8(orig)/9(rev1), 9(orig)/10(rev1), 10(orig)/12(rev1), 11(orig)/13(rev1), 12(orig)/14(rev1), 13(orig)/15(rev1) and string-compared them against the corresponding rev1 array positions: all 11 MATCH exactly, confirming design_notes.revision1_corrections' claim ('the other 10 original verification entries are byte-identical to revision 0' -- independently counted as 11, a harmless off-by-one in the generator's own prose, not a defect) is accurate.

FIX 1 (entry 7, revised) INDEPENDENTLY CONFIRMED NON-VACUOUS: re-ran the tightened python check (`'Plan of Record (2026-07-17)' in t and re.search(r'^##\\s+Track 2.*SUPERSEDED', t, re.M|re.I)`) against the live working tree -- PASSES (ROADMAP.md line 22 carries the literal banner heading '## Plan of Record (2026-07-17) -- Phase 005 Activated...'; line 92 carries '## Track 2 -- [SUPERSEDED as the phase-005 selection, 2026-07-17] Candidate Scope...', both conditions genuinely true). Independently re-ran the SAME two conditions directly against `git show HEAD:docs/planning/ROADMAP.md` (not merely trusting the contract's own new anti-vacuity entry 8's bash negation): zero matches for the literal banner string (`grep -c` = 0) and HEAD's Track-2 heading line reads '## Track 2 -- Phase 005 Candidate Scope: Consumer Reality & Enforcement Closure' with no SUPERSEDED marker -- both conditions independently confirmed FALSE at HEAD. This closes the round-1 HIGH finding: the tightened entry 7 discriminates correctly (fails pre-implementation, passes post-implementation) and is no longer satisfied by the six pre-existing 'phase 005 candidate scope' substrings that made the original bare `grep '005'` a false-pass.

FIX 1's new anti-vacuity companion (entry 8) independently re-run: PASSES on the live tree (correctly proves HEAD does not already satisfy the tightened marker), matching the entry-4/entry-15(orig-13) precedent pattern exactly. Not merely trusted -- the underlying two conditions were re-derived independently in the observation above using a separate `git show | grep -c` invocation, so entry 8's own bash negation logic is corroborated by an out-of-band check rather than taken on faith.

FIX 2 (new entry 11, .agent/ immutability guard) CONFIRMED LOAD-BEARING via two disposable, fully-reverted negative-control probes (repository confirmed byte-identical afterward): (a) appended a trailing newline to the HISTORICAL `.agent/qa/030.json` -- guard correctly FAILS ('immutable audit record(s) touched: M .agent/qa/030.json'); reverted with `git checkout -- .agent/qa/030.json`, `git diff --quiet HEAD -- .agent/qa/030.json` confirms byte-identical restoration. (b) created a throwaway untracked `.agent/contracts/999-probe.json` -- guard correctly FAILS ('?? .agent/contracts/999-probe.json'); removed, confirmed no residue via `git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all -- .agent/contracts`. The guard's own exclusions (`:(exclude).agent/contracts/031.json`, `:(exclude,glob).agent/qa/031*.json`) were also confirmed NOT to over-exclude: this review's own `.agent/qa/031-contract-review.json` (round 1) sits in the tree throughout and is correctly excluded by the `031*` glob without masking the historical-file probes above, so the guard's exclusion scope is precisely as narrow as intended.

FIX 2's scoping decision (checking only `.agent/qa` and `.agent/contracts`, deliberately NOT `.agent/run_state.json` or prior `feature_list_00N.json`) is confirmed to exactly mirror the cited precedent: re-read contracts 025 and 028's own equivalent verification entries -- both use the identical `.agent/qa .agent/contracts` path pair with no `run_state.json` or `feature_list_00N.json` inclusion, for the same stated reason (run_state.json is the orchestrator's own routinely-modified concern). This is a faithful application of house precedent, not a narrower-than-required workaround; the round-1 review's required_fix language explicitly treated `feature_list_00N.json` inclusion as optional ('if desired'), so its absence here is not a gap.

FIX 3 (entry 3, revised) CONFIRMED NON-VACUOUS: re-ran the strengthened check (Accepted + date + verbatim-quote + `Operator` keyword + email-shaped regex) against the live NIP file -- PASSES (Acceptance Record table row 'Operator | Ecosystem operator (`rosscn@nizamiq.com`)' satisfies both the keyword and the email-shaped pattern). Independently constructed a throwaway in-memory fixture string containing the three original substrings but NO operator/email content and re-ran the identical regex logic against it (not persisted to any file): correctly evaluates to 'would FAIL', confirming the strengthened entry 3 now genuinely requires operator attribution and closes the round-1 missing_acceptance_coverage gap.

FULL SUITE RE-RUN: extracted and executed all 15 verification commands programmatically (subprocess, not retyped) from the live .agent/contracts/031.json against the live working tree from the repository root -- all 15 exit 0 / PASS, including the two brand-new entries (7 revised, 8 new) and entry 11 (new). `python3 -c "jsonschema.validate(...)"` confirms .agent/contracts/031.json (revision 1) is schema-valid against schema/contract.schema.json.

REPOSITORY LEFT UNMODIFIED: `git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all` before and after this review shows the identical set of paths (3 modified: .agent/run_state.json, docs/planning/ROADMAP.md, docs/planning/manifest.json; 6 untracked: .agent/contracts/031.json, .agent/feature_list_005.json, .agent/product_spec_005.md, .agent/qa/031-contract-review.json, docs/nips/NIP-0001-ecosystem-engineering-cycle.md, v0.6.0-release-notes.md). All probe artifacts (bogus_out_of_scope_probe.txt equivalents, .agent/qa/030.json edit, .agent/contracts/999-probe.json) were created and removed/reverted within this same review session and leave no residue.

No new defect, vacuity, false-pass, or unsupported claim was found in revision 1. All three round-0 findings (entry 7 vacuity -- HIGH; missing .agent/ immutability guard -- MEDIUM; entry 3 operator-coverage gap -- non-blocking) are independently confirmed closed with fresh, reproducible evidence, not merely re-asserted from the generator's own design_notes.

final_verdict.summary: APPROVED at revision 1. All three round-0 findings are independently re-verified as closed, not merely trusted from the generator's design_notes.revision1_corrections claim: (1) the HIGH-severity false-pass in entry 7 is fixed -- the tightened check (literal 'Plan of Record (2026-07-17)' banner AND a Track-2-heading-anchored SUPERSEDED marker) passes on the live tree and independently fails against `git show HEAD:docs/planning/ROADMAP.md` (re-derived directly via grep -c and a Track-2 heading check, not merely via the contract's own new anti-vacuity entry 8, which itself also passes); (2) the MEDIUM-severity missing .agent/ immutability guard is added as new entry 11, exactly mirroring the established contracts 025/028 precedent, and confirmed genuinely load-bearing via two disposable, fully-reverted negative-control probes (a historical .agent/qa/030.json edit and a new untracked .agent/contracts/999-probe.json, both correctly caught and both cleanly reverted); (3) the non-blocking entry-3 operator-coverage gap is closed with an Operator-keyword + email-shaped-identity requirement, confirmed to pass on the real file and to fail on a fixture lacking that content. Structural JSON diff confirms `scope` and `non_goals` are byte-identical to revision 0, and 11 of the 13 original verification commands are unchanged verbatim -- only entries 3 and 7 were revised and two new entries (8, 11) added, exactly as claimed and nothing more. All 15 verification commands were re-extracted from the live contract and re-run from the repository root: all 15 PASS. The contract is schema-valid against schema/contract.schema.json. The repository was left byte-for-byte unmodified by this review (git status before/after identical; all probes reverted). Cleared to advance to Loop-2 implementation.
